subject: Mathematics
note: MathsUnit nodes for KS2. Auto-generated from cluster definitions. Each unit corresponds to one ConceptCluster, following the many-to-one domain mapping pattern used by other subjects.

units.name: Extend counting sequences in multiples of 4, 8, 50 and 100
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Counting in multiples of 4; Counting in multiples of 8; Counting in multiples of 50; Counting in multiples of 100.
units.pedagogical_rationale: These four skip-counting sequences are linked by their co_teach_hints (C001, C003, C004 mutually co-teach) and collectively prepare pupils for the 3, 4 and 8 times tables. They establish the entry point for three-digit number work.

units.name: Understand and apply place value in three-digit numbers
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Place value in three-digit numbers; Partitioning three-digit numbers; Finding 10 more and 10 less; Finding 100 more and 100 less.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Place value understanding (C007), partitioning (C008), and finding 10/100 more or less (C005, C006) are tightly bound by co_teach_hints. Together they build the core conceptual architecture for three-digit numbers.

units.name: Compare, order and estimate numbers to 1000
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Comparing and ordering numbers to 1000; Estimating numbers to 1000; Reading and writing numbers to 1000 in words.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Comparing/ordering, estimating, and reading/writing numbers in words all require a secure overall grasp of the size and representation of three-digit numbers. C010 co-teaches with C003 (scales link).

units.name: Add and subtract three-digit numbers mentally
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Mental addition with three-digit numbers; Mental subtraction with three-digit numbers.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Mental addition and subtraction with three-digit numbers are co-taught (C013 co-teaches with C012) and together establish fluency before formal methods are introduced.

units.name: Add and subtract using formal columnar methods and check with estimation
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Formal columnar addition; Formal columnar subtraction; Estimation and inverse operations in calculation.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Columnar addition, columnar subtraction and the use of estimation and inverse operations to check belong together as the formal written methods cluster. C016 is explicitly about using estimation and inverses to validate results.

units.name: Recall the 3, 4 and 8 times tables and understand their connections
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: 3 times table and related division facts; 4 times table and related division facts; 8 times table and related division facts; Connection between 2, 4 and 8 times tables (doubling).
units.pedagogical_rationale: The 3, 4 and 8 times tables are the Year 3 statutory recall targets. C020 (doubling connection between 2, 4 and 8) is explicitly co-taught with C018 and C019. These four form a coherent knowledge cluster.

units.name: Multiply and solve scaling and correspondence problems
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Written multiplication methods (2-digit × 1-digit); Scaling problems; Correspondence problems.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Written multiplication (2-digit × 1-digit), scaling problems and correspondence problems all extend times table knowledge into applied contexts. C022 and C023 both co-teach with table concepts.

units.name: Understand unit and non-unit fractions with small denominators
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Tenths as fractions and in place value; Unit fractions with small denominators; Non-unit fractions with small denominators.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Tenths (bridging fractions and place value), unit fractions and non-unit fractions together establish the conceptual foundation of the fraction number system in Year 3. All three mutually co-teach.

units.name: Compare, order and add fractions with the same denominator
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Equivalent fractions; Adding and subtracting fractions with the same denominator; Comparing and ordering fractions.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Equivalent fractions, adding/subtracting with the same denominator and comparing/ordering fractions are the procedural and relational skills built on the conceptual foundation. All three are mutually co-taught.

units.name: Measure in mixed units and calculate perimeter
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Measuring in mixed units (length, mass, volume); Perimeter of simple 2-D shapes.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Mixed-unit measurement and perimeter are both applied measurement skills that connect to number work and shape. They form the practical measurement cluster.

units.name: Calculate with money and read the time accurately
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Money calculations with £ and p; Time: Roman numerals I–XII and analogue clock; 24-hour clock; Comparing durations of time.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Money calculations, Roman numerals on clock faces, 24-hour clock and duration of time are co-taught as a cluster (C032, C034, C035 mutually co-teach; C033 co-teaches with C035). Time and money together form the real-world applications cluster.

units.name: Draw 2-D shapes and make 3-D shapes using measurements and properties
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Drawing 2-D shapes and making 3-D shapes; Horizontal, vertical, perpendicular and parallel lines.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Drawing/constructing shapes and identifying horizontal, vertical, perpendicular and parallel lines are both construction-oriented skills. C036 co-teaches with C039.

units.name: Identify and compare angles including right angles
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Angles as properties of shapes and as turns; Identifying right angles and comparing to other angles.
units.pedagogical_rationale: The concept of an angle and the identification of right angles versus acute/obtuse are closely related. C039 links back to these via co_teach_hints.

units.name: Interpret and construct scaled bar charts and pictograms
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Scaled bar charts and pictograms.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Scaled charts are the Year 3 entry point for statistics, requiring pupils to understand one symbol/bar represents more than one item. Foundational representation skill.

units.name: Solve one-step and two-step questions using information from data
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Solving one-step and two-step questions from data.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Moving from reading charts to reasoning from data and answering multi-step questions is the key statistical thinking progression in Year 3.

units.name: Understand place value and count in multiples with four-digit numbers
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Place value in four-digit numbers; Counting in multiples of 6, 7, 9, 25 and 1000.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Four-digit place value is the foundational concept; counting in multiples of 6, 7, 9, 25 and 1000 extends skip-counting to match the new times table demands. C002 co-teaches with C004.

units.name: Round to the nearest 10, 100 and 1000 and use negative numbers in context
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Negative numbers in context; Rounding to nearest 10, 100 and 1000.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Rounding and negative numbers both extend the number line concept beyond the positive integers. C004 co-teaches with C002.

units.name: Read Roman numerals to 100
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Roman numerals to 100 (I to C).
units.pedagogical_rationale: Roman numerals is a discrete knowledge strand within NC Number that does not co-teach with other Year 4 number concepts. Standalone practice cluster.

units.name: Add and subtract four-digit numbers using formal columnar methods
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Formal columnar addition and subtraction of four-digit numbers.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Only one concept in this domain. Four-digit columnar addition and subtraction (with exchange at multiple places) is the singular statutory focus and stands alone.

units.name: Recall all multiplication tables to 12 × 12 and use short multiplication
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: All multiplication tables to 12 × 12; Short multiplication (2- and 3-digit × 1-digit).
units.pedagogical_rationale: Complete times table recall is the major Year 4 milestone; short multiplication applies it to 2- and 3-digit numbers. C007 co-teaches with C008.

units.name: Investigate factor pairs and use commutativity in mental calculation
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Factor pairs and commutativity in mental calculation.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Factor pairs and commutativity are the conceptual tools that underpin flexible mental multiplication strategies — a distinct cluster from the recall/procedural focus.

units.name: Understand hundredths and extend decimal place value
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Hundredths and decimal place value; Decimal equivalents to common fractions.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Hundredths and decimal place value and the decimal equivalents of common fractions are co-taught (C010 and C012 mutually co-teach) and together establish the decimal number system.

units.name: Generate and use equivalent fractions
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Equivalent fractions using multiples and factors.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Generating equivalent fractions using multiples and factors is a distinct procedural skill that builds on Year 3 fraction understanding and underpins all fraction arithmetic to come.

units.name: Find the area of rectilinear shapes by counting and measuring
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Area of rectilinear shapes.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Area is the new measurement concept introduced in Year 4 and provides the conceptual entry point for the domain.

units.name: Convert between metric units of length, mass and volume
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Converting between metric units.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Metric conversion is a substantial and distinct procedural skill that applies place value understanding to measurement.

units.name: Classify triangles and quadrilaterals by their properties
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Classifying triangles and quadrilaterals.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Classification of triangles and quadrilaterals is the primary shape-properties focus in Year 4. Provides the vocabulary for symmetry work.

units.name: Identify and complete patterns with lines of symmetry
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Lines of symmetry.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Lines of symmetry are a distinct geometric concept best taught after classification, linking to the classification properties of shapes (e.g. equilateral triangles have 3 lines).

units.name: Describe and plot positions using coordinates in the first quadrant
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Coordinates in the first quadrant.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Only one concept in this domain. First-quadrant coordinate work is the complete Year 4 position/direction curriculum.

units.name: Read, interpret and draw line graphs showing continuous data over time
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Time graphs and continuous data.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Only one concept in this domain. Time graphs (line graphs) introduce continuous data and are the Year 4 statistics focus, distinct from the discrete data work of Y2-Y3.

units.name: Read, write and order numbers to 1,000,000 and round to any power of 10
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Numbers to 1,000,000 and their place value; Rounding to any power of 10.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Six-digit place value and rounding to any power of 10 (nearest 10,000; 100,000) are the two statutory Year 5 number/PV requirements. Only two concepts; single cluster appropriate.

units.name: Add and subtract whole numbers with more than four digits using mental and written strategies
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Mental and Written Addition and Subtraction Strategies.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Only one concept in this domain. Extension of addition and subtraction to larger numbers is the Year 5 focus, consolidating formal columnar methods.

units.name: Understand factors, multiples, primes, squares and cubes
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Prime numbers and composite numbers; Square numbers and cube numbers; Factors, common factors and multiples.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Prime/composite numbers and factors/multiples are mutually co-taught (C003 and C005 co-teach). Square and cube numbers naturally accompany this cluster as related number theory. Together they build multiplicative number sense before formal algorithms.

units.name: Multiply and divide using formal written methods
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Long multiplication (4-digit × 2-digit); Short division with remainders.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Long multiplication (4-digit × 2-digit) and short division with remainders are the two formal written method targets for Year 5. Both are high teaching-weight and procedurally distinct from the number theory cluster.

units.name: Convert between mixed numbers and improper fractions and add fractions with different denominators
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Adding and subtracting fractions with different denominators; Mixed numbers and improper fractions.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Mixed numbers/improper fractions and adding with different denominators are the procedural fraction targets that build on Year 3-4 equivalence work. Together they represent the Year 5 fraction arithmetic cluster.

units.name: Extend decimal place value to thousandths and understand percentages
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Thousandths and decimal place value to 3 decimal places; Percentages as fractions and decimals.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Decimal place value to three decimal places and the concept of percentage as parts per hundred are both representational knowledge concepts that expand the number system. Together they set up the fraction-decimal-percentage equivalence work.

units.name: Understand and calculate the volume of cuboids
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Volume of cuboids.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Only one concept in this domain. Volume of cuboids (V = l × w × h) is the new measurement concept introduced in Year 5.

units.name: Measure angles with a protractor and apply angle facts
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Measuring angles in degrees using a protractor; Angle facts (straight line, point, triangle).
units.pedagogical_rationale: Measuring angles in degrees and applying the three key angle facts (straight line = 180°, point = 360°, triangle = 180°) are taught together as a coherent angles cluster. Both use degrees and build directly on each other.

units.name: Reflect and translate shapes in all four quadrants of a coordinate grid
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Reflections and translations in all four quadrants.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Only one concept in this domain. Reflections and translations in all four quadrants extend Year 4 first-quadrant coordinate work to the full Cartesian plane.

units.name: Read and interpret graphs, tables and timetables to solve comparison problems
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: Reading and Interpreting Graphs, Tables and Timetables.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Only one concept in this domain. Year 5 statistics focuses on interpretation and reasoning across a variety of data representations including timetables.

units.name: Read, write and order whole numbers to 10,000,000
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C001.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Seven-digit place value is the Year 6 extension of place value and is the entry point for the domain before rounding and negative numbers.

units.name: Round to any degree of accuracy and calculate intervals across zero
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C002; MA-Y6-C003.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Rounding to any degree of accuracy and negative numbers/intervals across zero are co-taught (C002 co-teaches with C003) and together complete the Year 6 number system coverage.

units.name: Multiply and divide using formal long methods
units.subject: Mathematics
units.domain_name: Number - Addition, Subtraction, Multiplication and Division
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C004; MA-Y6-C005.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Long multiplication and long division are the two formal written method pinnacles of primary arithmetic. They represent the major Year 6 procedural target.

units.name: Apply order of operations and work with common factors, multiples and primes
units.subject: Mathematics
units.domain_name: Number - Addition, Subtraction, Multiplication and Division
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C006; MA-Y6-C007.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Order of operations (BIDMAS) and common factors/multiples/primes are the conceptual number theory targets of Year 6. C006 co-teaches with C029.

units.name: Develop mathematical fluency and apply problem-solving strategies
units.subject: Mathematics
units.domain_name: Number - Addition, Subtraction, Multiplication and Division
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C029; MA-Y6-C031.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Mathematical fluency with number and problem-solving strategies are cross-cutting process skills that permeate Year 6 mathematics. C029 is linked to C006; C031 represents the statutory problem-solving requirement.

units.name: Simplify, compare and add fractions with different denominators
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C008; MA-Y6-C009.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Simplifying/comparing fractions and adding/subtracting with different denominators are co-taught (C008 lists C009 in co_teach_hints). Together they consolidate fraction arithmetic.

units.name: Multiply and divide fractions and convert fluently between fractions, decimals and percentages
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C010; MA-Y6-C011.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Fraction multiplication/division and fraction-decimal-percentage equivalences are the upper primary fraction targets. C011 co-teaches with C008.

units.name: Understand and use ratio and proportion to solve problems
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C012.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Only one concept in this domain. Ratio and proportion is the new Year 6 domain that bridges to KS3 Ratio, Proportion and Rates of Change.

units.name: Use algebraic notation and formulae with letters for unknowns
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C013.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Algebraic notation and formulae is the gateway concept for Year 6 algebra, introducing the idea of using letters to represent unknowns. Must precede sequence and equation work.

units.name: Generate and describe linear sequences and solve equations in two variables
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C014; MA-Y6-C030.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Linear sequences, two-variable equations and mathematical reasoning/justification are co-taught (C014 and C030 mutually co-teach). Together they represent the Year 6 algebra application and reasoning cluster.

units.name: Calculate area and volume using formulae for 2-D and 3-D shapes
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C015; MA-Y6-C016.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Area of triangles and parallelograms and volume of cubes/cuboids are co-taught (C016 co-teaches with C015). Both apply the same principle of formula-based measurement and naturally belong together.

units.name: Know and apply angle facts in polygons and circles
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C017; MA-Y6-C018.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Angle facts in polygons and the parts of a circle are co-taught (C018 co-teaches with C017). They establish the shape-property knowledge before problem-solving application.

units.name: Identify properties of 3-D shapes and apply angle reasoning to solve problems
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C022; MA-Y6-C024.
units.pedagogical_rationale: 3-D shape properties/nets and applying angle facts to solve multi-step problems are co-taught with the angle-facts concept and represent the application tier of the domain.

units.name: Plot and describe coordinates in all four quadrants
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C019.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Four-quadrant coordinates extend Year 5 work and provide the spatial framework needed before transformations on the grid.

units.name: Perform and describe translations and reflections on a coordinate grid
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C023.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Translations and reflections on the full four-quadrant grid apply coordinate knowledge to geometric transformation, building on Year 5's work.

units.name: Construct and interpret pie charts and other statistical representations
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C020; MA-Y6-C028.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Pie charts (reading and calculating proportional sectors) and the broader skill of constructing/interpreting statistical charts are co-taught (C028 co-teaches with C020). Together they cover the representational strand.

units.name: Calculate and interpret averages including mean, median and mode
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C021; MA-Y6-C027.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Mean and the mode/median pair are co-taught (C021 and C027 mutually co-teach) and together provide a complete picture of central tendency at Year 6 level.

units.name: Describe and compare data distributions and use probability language
units.subject: Mathematics
units.description: Covers: MA-Y6-C026; MA-Y6-C025.
units.pedagogical_rationale: Describing distributions and introducing probability both require understanding likelihood and variation. Both co-teach with the averages cluster (C026 lists C020, C021, C027, C028; C025 is referenced by the averages cluster). Placed here as the analytical reasoning capstone.
